title: BioUtils: A Case Study in Transcriptomic Analysis of Renal Cell Carcinoma
text: BioUtils uses limma (Linear Models Microarray data) framework differential expression analysis, solves problems elegantly: Rather testing gene independently, limma fits linear model probes simultaneously. key innovation empirical Bayes moderation: borrows variance information across genes produce stabilized estimate within-group variability probe. Genes samples noisy measurements benefit pooled information, prevents genes accidentally small variance estimates generating artificially inflated t-statistics. result, called TopTable, data frame one row per probe, sorted evidence differential expression. important columns : logFC - log2 fold change RCC normal tissue. value 2 means RCC samples express gene 4-fold higher average. value -1 means expression halved RCC. adj.P.Val - p-value Benjamini-Hochberg correction multiple testing. controls false discovery rate (FDR): adj.P.Val 0.05 means expect 5% probes call significant false positives, rather 5% tests wrong.

title: BioUtils: A Case Study in Transcriptomic Analysis of Renal Cell Carcinoma
text: analyze.gene() integrates multiple statistical perspectives single object. worth understanding component contributes: adaptive t-test (result$raw$parametric): BioUtils first runs var.test() check whether two groups equal variances. (common occurrence gene expression data, disease states often increase expression variability), automatically uses WelchтАЩs t-test, assume equal variances, rather standard StudentтАЩs t-test. prevents common source inflated false positives. CohenтАЩs d (result$effect.size): p-value alone tell whether statistically significant difference biologically meaningful. 17 samples, even tiny difference means can reach statistical significance. CohenтАЩs d standardized effect size, difference group means divided pooled standard deviation, puts magnitude difference context. d 0.2 small; 0.5 moderate; 0.8 large. clinical context, genes small effect sizes unlikely serve robust biomarkers even highly significant. Bootstrapped confidence interval (result$confidence.interval): confidence interval around CohenтАЩs d quantifies uncertainty true effect size given sample size. narrow CI (e.g., [0.8, 1.2]) means precise estimate; wide CI (e.g., [0.1, 1.5]) means true effect anywhere negligible large, cautious strong claims. Wilcoxon rank-sum test (result$nonparametric.p): t-test assumes expression values roughly normally distributed within group. Gene expression data often violates assumption, particularly one group outlier samples. Wilcoxon test makes distributional assumptions provides independent check. tests agree (result$robustness), can confident result. disagree, t-test significant, Wilcoxon , result may driven distributional differences rather genuine shift population mean.

result <- analyze.gene(df.single) cat(result$interpretation) #> The difference is statistically significant with a large effect size. #> Result is robust across parametric and nonparametric tests. #> Potentially biologically meaningful. # Inspect individual components cat("Effect size (Cohen's d):", round(result$effect.size, 3), "
") #> Effect size (Cohen's d): 2.312 cat("Effect size class:      ", result$effect.size.class, "

text: two important columns GSEA output : NES (Normalized Enrichment Score): core statistic GSEA. positive NES means gene set enriched among genes upregulated RCC, genes belonging pathway tend cluster top ranked list. negative NES means pathway enriched among downregulated genes, suppressed RCC relative normal tissue. normalization accounts gene set size, NES values comparable across sets. padj: NES tested significance permuting gene labels recomputing enrichment scores null hypothesis genes randomly distributed ranked list. padj resulting p-value Benjamini-Hochberg correction across tested pathways. leadingEdge: subset genes pathway contribute driving enrichment score. biologically interesting genes within enriched pathway, ones тАЬleading edgeтАЭ ranked list. strong candidates follow-analyze.gene(). closes analytical loop: GSEA identifies biological programs dysregulated RCC pathway level, leading edge genes point us back individual-gene tools, analyze.gene() gene.analysis.plot(), characterize important drivers within pathway.

title: Transcriptomic Signatures of Renal Cell Carcinoma: A Visual Analytics Case Study
text: Cancer biology fundamentally problem gene expression. cell becomes malignant, simply acquire mutation stop , undergoes wholesale reorganization transcriptional state, activating programs promote uncontrolled growth silencing regulatory mechanisms normally restrain . Understanding exactly genes affected, much, coordinated patterns central task cancer transcriptomics. analysis addresses following research question: transcriptomic changes characterize clear cell renal cell carcinoma, can small gene expression signature reliably distinguish tumor normal kidney tissue? Three specific objectives follow question. First, identify individual genes significantly dysregulated RCC relative normal kidney tissue characterize magnitude statistical robustness differences. Second, determine whether differentially expressed genes cluster coherent biological pathways, particularly consistent known RCC molecular biology. Third, evaluate whether sparse, LASSO-selected gene panel can serve candidate diagnostic signature, establishing connection population-level transcriptomics potential clinical utility.

title: Transcriptomic Signatures of Renal Cell Carcinoma: A Visual Analytics Case Study
text: Renal cell carcinoma (RCC) common malignancy kidney, accounting approximately 90 percent kidney cancers estimated 80,000 new diagnoses annually United States. clear cell subtype, represents roughly 75 percent RCC cases, defined molecular level biallelic inactivation VHL (Von Hippel-Lindau) tumor suppressor gene chromosome 3p. VHL normally targets transcription factor HIF1╬▒\alpha (Hypoxia Inducible Factor 1-alpha) ubiquitin-mediated degradation normoxic conditions. VHL lost, HIF1╬▒\alpha constitutively accumulates regardless oxygen availability, driving broad transcriptional program mimics chronic hypoxia. program activates genes involved angiogenesis (particularly VEGF receptors), anaerobic glucose metabolism, cell survival, processes collectively favor tumor growth invasion. Despite well-characterized molecular etiology, RCC frequently diagnosed advanced stage early disease largely asymptomatic. тАЬincidentalomaтАЭ pattern, discovery imaging performed unrelated reasons, accounts growing proportion diagnoses, many patients still present metastatic disease, five-year survival rates remain 15 percent. Identifying robust molecular signatures bulk tumor expression data potential improve early detection, subtype classification, patient stratification targeted therapy. dataset used analysis, GDS507 NCBI Gene Expression Omnibus, contains expression profiles 17 clear cell RCC tumor samples 10 matched normal kidney cortex samples, measured Affymetrix HG-U133A microarray platform (~22,000 probe sets). patients underwent radical partial nephrectomy, tumor normal samples collected surgical specimen possible, reducing inter-patient confounding. central hypothesis threefold: (1) clear cell RCC produce large, statistically robust differential expression signature relative normal kidney; (2) signature enriched gene sets related hypoxia, angiogenesis, metabolic reprogramming consistent HIF1╬▒\alpha pathway activation; (3) small number genes, fewer 20, jointly carry sufficient discriminative information classify tumor normal tissue high accuracy.

title: Fit LASSO Regression for Multi-Gene Biomarker Discovery тАФ fit.lasso
text: Applies cross-validated LASSO logistic regression identify sparse set genes jointly predict binary phenotype. Unlike univariate tests, LASSO accounts joint contribution genes simultaneously, automatically penalizing redundant predictors.

text: expression.matrix Numeric matrix gene expression values returned extract.expression()$expression. Rows probes, columns samples. matrix transposed internally samples become rows required glmnet. phenotype.vector Factor integer vector binary phenotype labels, one per sample (e.g., 0 control, 1 disease). Must length number columns expression.matrix. alpha Numeric. Elastic net mixing parameter. 1 (default) gives pure LASSO; 0 gives Ridge; values 0 1 give elastic net. nfolds Integer. Extract selected genes coef(fit, s = "lambda.1se"); probes non-zero coefficients LASSO-selected biomarkers.

title: Fit LASSO Regression for Multi-Gene Biomarker Discovery тАФ fit.lasso
text: LASSO suited high-dimensional genomic data number genes far exceeds samples. shrinks coefficients exactly zero, retaining genes independent predictive value. complements run.limma.de() - limma ranks genes individual differential expression, LASSO identifies minimal subset non-redundant joint predictive signal.

what: Run Gene Set Enrichment Analysis тАФ run.gsea
title: Run Gene Set Enrichment Analysis тАФ run.gsea
text: Tests whether predefined gene sets (e.g., pathways GO terms) systematically enriched among genes ranked differential expression, using fgseaMultilevel algorithm fgsea.

title: Run Gene Set Enrichment Analysis тАФ run.gsea
code: run.gsea(de.results, genes, pathways, min.size = 15, max.size = 500)

title: Run Gene Set Enrichment Analysis тАФ run.gsea
text: de.results Data frame returned run.limma.de(). Must contain logFC column. Row names probe IDs. genes Data frame gene annotations returned extract.expression()$gene. Must contain columns "ID" probe identifiers "Gene symbol" gene symbols matching format used pathway databases MSigDB. pathways Named list character vectors, element gene set names pathway labels. MSigDB GO gene sets format can loaded via msigdbr. min.size Integer. Minimum number genes required gene set tested. Default 15. max.size Integer. Maximum gene set size. Default 500.

title: Run Gene Set Enrichment Analysis тАФ run.gsea
text: data frame one row per tested pathway, containing: pathway Gene set name. pval Nominal p-value. padj BH-adjusted p-value across tested pathways. NES Normalized enrichment score. Positive values indicate enrichment among upregulated genes; negative values indicate enrichment among downregulated genes. size Number genes pathway present ranked list. leadingEdge List-column genes driving enrichment score.

title: Run Gene Set Enrichment Analysis тАФ run.gsea
text: Uses fgseaMultilevel algorithm, estimates p-values using adaptive multilevel splitting Monte Carlo approach. accurate original permutation-based fgseaSimple require fixed permutation count. GSEA operates full ranked gene list run.limma.de() rather filtered subset, preserving information genes. Probe IDs TopTable row names resolved gene symbols via genes annotation data frame matching pathway gene sets. Probes gene annotation silently dropped ranked list, matched pathway. leadingEdge genes strong candidates follow-analyze.gene() gene.analysis.plot().

what: Run Differential Expression Analysis Using limma тАФ run.limma.de
title: Run Differential Expression Analysis Using limma тАФ run.limma.de
text: Performs genome-wide differential expression analysis across probes using limma linear modeling framework. recommended approach microarray data GEO, borrows variance information across genes produce stable estimates gene--gene t-tests.

title: Run Differential Expression Analysis Using limma тАФ run.limma.de
code: run.limma.de(geo, condition.col = "disease.state", adjust.method = "BH")

title: Run Differential Expression Analysis Using limma тАФ run.limma.de
text: geo Named list returned extract.expression(), containing minimum $expression (probe x sample matrix) $phenotype (sample metadata data frame). condition.col Character. Name column geo$phenotype use grouping variable. Default "disease.state". adjust.method Character. P-value correction method passed limma::topTable(). Default "BH" (Benjamini-Hochberg FDR).

title: Run Differential Expression Analysis Using limma тАФ run.limma.de
text: data frame (TopTable) one row per probe, sorted evidence differential expression, containing: logFC Log2 fold-change conditions. AveExpr Average log2 expression across samples. t Moderated t-statistic. P.Value Raw p-value. adj.P.Val FDR-adjusted p-value (BH default). B Log-odds gene differentially expressed.

title: Run Differential Expression Analysis Using limma тАФ run.limma.de
text: Unlike running analyze.gene() probe individually, limma fits linear model probes simultaneously applies empirical Bayes shrinkage variance estimates. stabilizes results genes observations standard method microarray DE analysis. returned TopTable primary input downstream functions volcano.plot() run.gsea(), can filtered adj.P.Val logFC thresholds define gene signature.

what: Plot Volcano Plot from Differential Expression Results тАФ volcano.plot
title: Plot Volcano Plot from Differential Expression Results тАФ volcano.plot
text: Generates volcano plot visualizing relationship statistical significance fold-change magnitude probes differential expression analysis. Points colored whether exceed user-defined significance fold-change thresholds.

code: volcano.plot(de.results, fc.threshold = 1, fdr.threshold = 0.05)

title: Plot Volcano Plot from Differential Expression Results тАФ volcano.plot
text: de.results Data frame returned run.limma.de(), containing minimum columns logFC adj.P.Val. fc.threshold Numeric. Absolute log2 fold-change cutoff labeling genes differentially expressed. Default 1 (.e., 2-fold). fdr.threshold Numeric. Adjusted p-value cutoff. Default 0.05.

title: Plot Volcano Plot from Differential Expression Results тАФ volcano.plot
text: ggplot object showing probe point logFC x-axis -log10(adj.P.Val) y-axis. Points colored upregulated, downregulated, significant. Dashed threshold lines drawn specified cutoffs.

text: volcano plot standard summary visualization TopTable run.limma.de() serves genome-wide complement gene.analysis.plot(), visualizes single gene. Probes appear upper corners (high fold-change, low FDR) strongest candidates follow-analyze.gene() inclusion gene signature run.gsea().
